description_long: This object is a vintage, box-shaped television with a wooden exterior casing and a large, convex glass screen dominating the front. The front panel features a metallic control section on the right, equipped with four rotary knobs and a circular channel selector dial, all set within a brushed metal frame. The top and sides are finished in a worn, brown wood texture, showing signs of age and use. The back panel is made of dark, vented metal with visible screws, ventilation grilles, and a power cord extending from the lower left. The television is bulky and heavy, typical of mid-20th-century CRT models, and is designed to rest on a flat surface. Its overall appearance suggests durability and a utilitarian design, with minimal decorative elements.
description: A vintage, boxy television with a wooden exterior, large convex glass screen, and metallic control panel featuring rotary knobs and a channel dial.
description_view_0: Front view showing the large convex glass screen, metallic control panel with four knobs and a channel dial on the right.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the plain wooden side panel and the protruding power cord at the back.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing the dark, vented metal back panel with ventilation grilles, screws, and the power cord.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the plain wooden side panel with no visible controls or features.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick, brown wooden bezel with a metallic section on the right; utilitarian and robust with no decorative features.
attribute_control_button_layout: Cluster of four rotary knobs and a circular channel dial on the front right panel.
